title: Key concepts: financial institutions
description: Check your knowledge of the history of banking!

hints: Credit involves lending money to someone else.

choices.content: providing upfront funding for expensive trading ventures
forcing traders to invest their own money in their trading ventures
by eliminating the risks of long-distance travel

hints: Banking developed in part _because_ of interactions between cultures.

choices.content: originated in Medieval Italy, but drew on knowledge from other cultures
developed independently around the same time in China, the Middle East, and Europe

hints: Usury was considered a sin in some form by Judaism, Christianity, and Islam.

choices.content: accepted as a normal business practice by Judaism, Christianity, and Islam
earning a profit from charging interest on loans

hints: A bill of exchange was a financial document, not a financial practice.

choices.content: was considered usury by the Church
entitled the holder to a specified payment from a third party at a later date
was a receipt documenting the activity of a completed trading voyage

hints: Financial ideas and practices existed long before Fibonacci’s lifetime.

choices.content: explained how he had invented the concepts of finance and banking
documented his father’s work as a customs official
took useful mathematical concepts developed in India and the Middle East and explicitly applied them to commercial transactions
